Module 4:
Your Pre-Prelaunch, Part 1
!
Executive Summary
It’s time to talk about the pre-prelaunch, which we like to call the “pre-pre”.
This happens before the prelaunch, and most of the time people don’t even
have a hint of what’s coming or what you’re leading into.
This is where you really finalize your product and offer. You use it to identify
objections, gather testimonials, warm your list, and even attract partners.
It’s ultimately about creating buzz around your launch.
We usually start with what I call the Shot Across the Bow - old naval term
for when they would fire a shot in front of the other boat to warn them that
something is coming. The essence is to let them know something is coming
but not try to sell them. The message is that the product is not quite ready
yet.
A big thing to keep in mind is that you want to avoid the “red zone” of
people’s BS detectors. When there’s no hint of a pitch and you're excited
about releasing something you get good response - it’s much softer.
!
!
!
!

-- 1 of 3 --

The characteristics of buzz
• 	In my market (Internet Marketing), everyone is highly connected
• 	Lots of forums
• 	Lots of social media
• 	People are on all the email lists
• 	All this needs to be taken into account when I do my pre-prelaunch
• 	People are not interested in sharing your sale or advertising with people
• 	It’s important to be authentic and create value - the more you do this the
quicker the buzz will spread
• 	I like to hit multiple modalities - talk about it on stage, on social media,
different places
• 	Think of this in terms of Concentric Circles
• 	PLF and my team in the middle - people closest to me
• 	Closest people to me are PLF Owners - usually the first hint comes
to this group
• 	Joint Venture Partners
• 	Social media following - inherently very shareable
• 	Prospect list
• 	People on JV’s lists
!
2	© 2014 Internet Alchemy, Inc.

-- 2 of 3 --

• 	Market in general
• 	Rest of the world
• 	People like to support that which they help create
• 	“I’m almost done creating this product but can you tell me what your
number one question/issue is with XYZ?”
• 	Ask people for feedback in your market - what do they need to learn to
really change their lives?
• 	Once they get involved and feel like they’re helping you create the
product they are more likely to support, defend, and get involved in
pre-launch process
• 	A little mystery in your pre-pre is good - get people to try and piece
together what’s coming
• 	In the book The Tipping Point by Malcolm Gladwell
• 	Mavens are people that love to collect information
• 	Connectors are people that are active in social media
• 	These are the people you should look for
• 	Persons of influence
• 	Who has real influence and would be good to get involved?
• 	People love scarce information and love to tell people about it
!
